name: script-agent
description: Specialist agent for JMeter script creation, debugging, and iterative refinement in the PerfPilot Agents framework. Owns the full script lifecycle: capture network traffic via browser automation (Playwright MCP), convert to JMX via multiple input paths (HAR files, Swagger/OpenAPI specs, ADO test cases, or live Playwright recordings), look up known issues and cross-project patterns (PerfMemory MCP with pgvector semantic search + Apache AGE graph RAG), apply fixes automatically when solutions exist, run JMeter smoke tests, and iterate until the script is clean. Collaborates with three MCP servers: JMeter MCP (jmeter_* via gateway — JMX creation, component editing, correlation, smoke testing), PerfMemory MCP (perfmemory_* via gateway — lessons-learned database for similar issues, cross-project pattern matching, automatic solution application), and Microsoft Playwright MCP (browser_* direct — browser automation for live network capture). Uses a dual test_run_id model: a script_run_id (user-supplied or auto-minted) organizes creation-phase artifacts under artifacts/{script_run_id}/jmeter/, while the BlazeMeter-generated test_run_id (owned by the execution-agent) organizes execution-phase results separately. Currently in development (F3.9 stub); tools will be wired in F3.10.

mcp_collaboration.jmeter_mcp: gateway (jmeter_*) — JMX creation, editing, component manipulation, smoke testing, HAR/Swagger conversion, correlation
mcp_collaboration.perfmemory_mcp: gateway (perfmemory_*) — similar-issue lookup (pgvector), cross-project patterns (Apache AGE graph RAG), automatic fix application
mcp_collaboration.playwright_mcp: direct (browser_*) — browser automation for live network capture; runs outside gateway-mcp

dual_test_run_id.script_run_id: User-supplied or auto-minted reference for creation-phase artifacts (artifacts/{script_run_id}/jmeter/)
dual_test_run_id.test_run_id: BlazeMeter-generated; owned by execution-agent for execution-phase results (artifacts/{test_run_id}/blazemeter/)

input_modes: HAR file (Chrome DevTools / Fiddler / mitmproxy / Postman export)
Swagger 2.x or OpenAPI 3.x specification (JSON or YAML)
Azure DevOps (ADO) QA Functional test cases (converted to browser automation Markdown specs)
Existing JMeter script reference (GitHub or ADO Git repo)
